**JOB PURPOSE:** 




 The role is primarily responsible for executing the GTx strategy of Oman, working with FAB CEO in Oman and FAB Head of coverage Oman in leading developments and sales, structuring GTx solutions, managing and growing of trade and cash business for FAB across Oman local clients in\-country, outbound and inbound business originating from our network.
 


* The job is critical in channeling and executing GTx HO strategy into Oman.
* The role is based in Muscat (Oman)



  





**KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES:** 



* Setting and executing the strategy for the business, assess the market opportunities and challenges, and overall parameters of the business, in line with Head of GTx GCC ex UAE and Head of GTx International



  





**Job Context:** 




**Specific Accountability** 




 As Executive Director Head of GTx Oman, you will be responsible for:
 



  




* Actively engage with clients together with the Head of coverage, Oman and Oman Coverage team to build GTx business for the Bank in the country. A strong drive for GTx Sales, understanding of the GTx Products and clients in Oman.



  




* Delivery of specific revenue targets returns on risk adjusted assets and equity consistent with the Bank’s acceptable risk parameters. Keeping the management aware of performance gaps with suggestions to rectify and improve performance and deepen client relationship



  




* Structuring, executing and implementing workable and innovative solutions that meet customer needs. Providing technical expertise to relationship managers and facilitating client discussions. Lead structuring of bespoke GTx Solutions and ensure high\-quality customer experience through consultative approach



  




* Establishing strong stakeholder relationships and work with colleagues and partners across the network to build a strong GTx Franchise in Oman. Work closely with Cash Management and Trade Finance Product Management team in UAE to finalize related documentation/ structures/ rollout new products/ solutions/policies. Provide Oman country / competition feedback to review Bank’s product offering in Oman and potential up\-tiering of the product suite.



  




* Working closely with Coverage teams in\-country and network to develop markets, clients, and products based on detailed Client Account Plan for growing GTx business and maximizing market share. Partnering with coverage teams to develop quality credit proposals supporting structuring of existing and new facilities



  




* Ensuring transactions, facilities and implementation are being executed in a timely manner. Working closely with Compliance, Finance, Implementation, Operations, etc. for client on\-boarding and smooth execution of transactions



  




* Identifying and managing fraud, regulatory and compliance risks in the transactions when developing solutions for clients. Proactively managing transactional risk in an efficient and profitable manner.



  




* Identifying opportunities for continuous improvement of products, systems, processes, and practices while considering client needs, market developments and trends. Actively participate in opportunities to update product skills, understand market trends and competitor behaviors



  




* Understanding and complying with regulations and internal policies i.e. Credit/ Compliance/ Operational Risks, etc.



  





**FRAMEWORKS, BOUNDARIES, \& DECISION\-MAKING AUTHORITY:** 



* Strong Client network with focus to grow business for the bank in the country. Strong origination capability coupled with excellent customer service \& negotiation skills
* Strong technical knowledge in GTx products including Cash Management, Channels and Trade Finance
* Self\-starter who can handle all enquiries independently and able to effectively interact with customer to resolve enquiries.
* Strong interpersonal skills as well as a team player with strong influential skill that can motivate other team members to achieve the business objective. Ability to work in Matrix environment
* Self\-confident and performance driven. Ability to work under pressure and with a strong delivery focus.
* Ability to articulate, formulate and deliver high quality client presentation
* Understanding of operations processes and procedures, and related risk in trade transactions
* Familiar with the regulatory environment concerning financial industry in country
* Strong written and spoken English (Arabic is a plus)



  





**QUALIFICATIONS \& EXPERIENCE:** 




**Minimum Qualification:** 
 Degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Commerce or equivalent discipline
 



**Minimum Experience: 15\+** 
 years Transaction Banking experience.
 



**Omani National**

data.title: External Affairs & Partnerships Manager - (Oman)
data.raw_snippet:
**Purpose/ Mission:**
 The External Affairs \& Partnership Manager will lead all government relations, stakeholder engagement, and strategic partnerships for Meranti Green Steel in Oman. This role is critical in positioning the company as a key player in Oman’s industrial and energy transition ecosy
data.jd_text:
**Purpose/ Mission:**
 The External Affairs \& Partnership Manager will lead all government relations, stakeholder engagement, and strategic partnerships for Meranti Green Steel in Oman. This role is critical in positioning the company as a key player in Oman’s industrial and energy transition ecosystem, while securing strong relationships with government entities, regulators, investors, and strategic partners. The successful candidate will act as the company’s “external bridge,” ensuring alignment with national priorities, facilitating approvals, and unlocking long\-term value through partnerships across the green steel, hydrogen, and energy value chain.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities:**
**Government \& Regulatory Affairs**
* Develop and execute a comprehensive government engagement strategy
* Build and maintain strong relationships with:
* Ministries (Energy, Commerce \& Industry, Environment, Investment)
* Regulatory authorities and free zone entities
* Support:
* Licensing, approvals, and policy alignment
* Advocacy on regulatory and policy matters
* Monitor and analyze
* Policy developments impacting green steel, hydrogen, and industrial sectors


**Strategic Partnerships \& Ecosystem Development**
* Identify, develop, and manage strategic partnerships across:
* Energy suppliers (renewables, hydrogen)
* Offtakers and industrial customers
* Infrastructure and logistics providers
* Lead negotiations and structuring of:
* MoUs / partnership agreements
* Strategic collaboration frameworks
* Support development of an integrated green steel ecosystem in Oman


**Stakeholder Engagement \& Positioning**
* Develop and implement a stakeholder engagement plan:
* Government
* Investors
* Local communities
* Industry associations
* Position Meranti Green Steel as a thought leader in:
* Decarbonization
* Green industrial development
* Represent the company in:
* Conferences, forums, and high\-level meetings


**Corporate Affairs \& Communications (Strategic Layer)**
* Work with internal teams to align:
* External messaging
* Public positioning
* Support:
* Strategic communications
* ESG and sustainability narratives
* Ensure consistency of messaging with national and international stakeholders


**Project Development Support**
* Support project milestones by:
* Facilitating government coordination
* Accelerating approvals and stakeholder alignment
* Act as a bridge between:
* Internal teams (legal, project, commercial)
* External stakeholders


**Support the Director Operations in all relevant tasks where requested.**
**Requirements/ Skills/ Experience:**
* 10–15\+ years of experience in:
* Government relations
* External affairs
* Strategic partnerships
* Strong experience in:
* Large\-scale industrial, energy, or infrastructure projects
* Deep understanding of:
* Oman / GCC regulatory and business environment


**Established network with government and industry stakeholders in Oman is highly preferred**
**Required Education/ Qualification/ Training:**
 Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in: Business, Economics, Political Science, International Relations, or related field
   

  

**Required Language Proficiency:**
 English \- Fluent

data.title: Software Engineering Director
data.raw_snippet:
This is the general track for Engineering Director at Canonical, apply here if you are confident to run a project which spans teams and time zones, hiring and managing engineering managers and staff engineers.
   

  

 Canonical's largest software products are built by multiple teams working togeth
data.jd_text:
This is the general track for Engineering Director at Canonical, apply here if you are confident to run a project which spans teams and time zones, hiring and managing engineering managers and staff engineers.
   

  

 Canonical's largest software products are built by multiple teams working together under a single director of engineering. Once you have passed the first round of interviews we will discuss specific software products which would be a good fit for your interests.
   

  

 We believe that open source is just starting to transform the tech sector and enterprise compute. Our goal is to make open source easier, more reliable and more secure for deployment and development. We strive to be the provider of 'most software to most companies'. To deliver on that ambition, our engineers are carefully selected from the applicants across the globe. We select for brilliance and motivation to take open source to the next level. Our engineering directors set the pace and the direction to help teams achieve more than they realised they could, and feel proud of the result.
   

  

 Even though this is a senior management position, managing managers, we expect Engineering Directors to be outstanding developers with the confidence to lead by example across the full range of activities in their teams. You need to understand what great code looks like in the language and genre of your product \- whether that is a web service, or a system daemon, or a desktop application, or a distributed system. You'll need to know how to drive testing and benchmarking to improve the clarity in your teams around quality and performance. You'll also need to be confident to set and defend high expectations of work ethic, consistency, focus and learning.
   

  

 We have director level roles across a wide range of engineering domains, including:
   

  

* Python and Golang
* C / C\+\+ / Rust
* Data infrastructure
* HTML / CSS / JavaScript / Typescript / React
* Flutter
* Distro packaging and systems
* SAAS and web microservices
* Kernel
* Servers
* Graphics, Browser and Desktop
* Silicon enablement and embedded devices
* Product Security


 If your domain of expertise isn't listed above, yet you feel it's relevant to Canonical, then feel free to apply anyway. We will route you to the most suitable team.
   

  

 Location: The role is remote, with positions available in all time zones. It includes a requirement for global travel for twice per year, for trips up to two weeks long.
   

  

 What you'll do
   

  

* Lead multiple teams of engineers, ranging from graduate to senior
* Develop your engineering managers and maintain Canonical culture
* Hire staff level engineers for specific roles like performance and quality
* Coach, mentor, and offer career development feedback
* Identify and measure indicators of team health and productivity
* Ensure rounded delivery including community participation and docs
* Implement disciplined engineering processes and defend them
* Set and measure progress against benchmark goals for speed and efficiency
* Represent your product to stakeholders, partners, and customers
* Develop and evangelise great engineering and organisational practices
* Plan and manage progress on agreed goals and projects
* Be an active part of the leadership team, collaborating with other leaders
* Collaborate with leads for related or dependent products


 What we're looking for in you
   

  

* An exceptional academic track record from both high school and university
* Undergraduate degree in Computer Science or STEM, or a compelling narrative about your alternative path
* Extensive management experience and confidence to set high expectations
* Outstanding Linux based software engineering track record
* Drive and a track record of going above\-and\-beyond expectations
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English
* Public speaking and presentation skills
* A love of developing and growing people and a track record of it
* Experience in leading, coaching and mentoring software developers
* Organised and able to ensure your team delivers timely, high quality results
* Well\-organised, self\-starting and able to deliver to schedule
* Professional manner interacting with colleagues, partners, and community
* You have advanced expertise in your own domain
* You are knowledgeable and passionate about software development
* You have solid experience working in an agile development environment
* You have a demonstrated drive for continual learning
* Builds trust, relationships and confidence
* Result\-oriented, with a personal drive to meet commitments
* Ability to travel twice a year, for company events up to two weeks each


**Additional Skills We Value**
* Experience in a developer advocacy or community role
* Open source community engagement and leadership
* Ops and system administration experience
* Performance engineering and security experience


 What we offer you
   

  

 We consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognise outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance\-driven annual bonus. We provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.
   

  

* Distributed work environment with twice\-yearly team sprints in person
* Personal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per year
* Annual compensation review
* Recognition rewards
* Annual holiday leave
* Maternity and paternity leave
* Employee Assistance Programme
* Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues
* Priority Pass, and travel upgrades for long haul company events


**About Canonical**
 Canonical is a pioneering tech firm at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open source projects and the platform for AI, IoT and the cloud, we are changing the world on a daily basis. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence \- in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Canonical has been a remote\-first company since its inception in 2004\. Working here is a step into the future, and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game.
   

  

 Canonical is an equal opportunity employer
   

  

 We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

data.title: Head of  BOS Integrated Services Hub 1
data.raw_snippet:
**About This Opportunity**
 Ericsson is looking for a Head of Integration Services Hub1 within Service Line (SL) Business and Operations Systems (BOS) as a key leadership role part of BOS Delivery Excellence, responsible for leading the service excellence. The role focuses on services business pre\-
data.jd_text:
**About This Opportunity**
 Ericsson is looking for a Head of Integration Services Hub1 within Service Line (SL) Business and Operations Systems (BOS) as a key leadership role part of BOS Delivery Excellence, responsible for leading the service excellence. The role focuses on services business pre\-sales and overlooks integrated delivery models setup including System Integration, Application Development \& Maintenance , and IT Managed Services.
   

  

 This role reports to the Head of SL BOS Delivery Excellence for MEMA and will be part of BOS Delivery Excellence Leadership team.
   

  

 What You Will Do:
   

  

* Lead BOS service excellence, managing team of senior services professionals.
* Accountable for services engagement and pre\-sales including target topline and profitability.
* Responsible for aligning with Solution Area on Services GTM and drive services offering build up , presales and delivery engagements for the EMEA region.
* Secure service delivery readiness through competence planning inline with business and portfolio evolution.
* Senior People Manager of own unit and a senior Leader across matrixed organization extending to solution line professionals in other CU BOS, BOS solution lines and SD/SDU units.
* Adopt, position and expand integrated service delivery offerings; with focus on customer value realization, delivery efficiency, and adapting to different commercial models.
* Champion delivery efficiency initiatives in collaboration with program management, CU BOS SL and SD/SDU.
* Set Individual Performance and Development goals for members with regular check\-ins.
* Responsible for team dimensioning; and Cost Center Responsible to deliver over\-Absorption target and required Utilization/Billability.
* Drive a Knowledge Sharing and Asset building culture across their community.
* Instill a culture of add\-on sales and customer value maximization.


 What You Will Bring:
   

  

* 12\+ years of experience in telecom industry with leadership track record in professional services including system integration, application development \& maintenance, and IT managed services.
* Deep understanding of BOS portfolio including products and services.
* University degree in ICT/Engineering. A graduate degree such as an MBA is a plus.
* Strong People Manager track record leading Senior Team members and leading Leaders.
* Deep Telecom knowledge including understanding of Telecom networks, OSS \& BSS components and familiarity with TmForum (eTOM, SID, TAM)
* Advanced experience with IT Systems and Integration
* OSS and BSS full stack integrations and surrounding ecosystems
* API management \& microservice architecture
* Cloud\-Native \& Platform transformation
* Data analytics, AI/ML applications in service operations
* In depth knowledge of different SDLC methodologies including Agile, Waterfall and DevOps approaches.
* Proven experience with MS IT and ADM related frameworks – ITIL, COBIT \& SAFe
* Demonstrated experience leading cross\-functional highly matrixed and globally distributed teams.
* Recommended certifications (external): ITIL, Lean Six Sigma, PMP
* Recommended certifications (internal): MSCOO, ICT PD JS8 GIAP certification
* Strong consultative, presentation, and communication skills
* Proven track record to deliver results within a time sensitive and high\-pressure environment.
* Highly proficient collaboration skills
* Solid ability to communicate effectively at executive levels.
* In\-depth knowledge of running Organizational and Solution Level Governance.
* Fluent in English; additional languages are a plus.


 Note: There are a total of 4 Head of Integration Services Hub vacancies with the same responsibility and skills requirements.
   

  

 Ericsson South Africa recruits in line with its Employment Equity plan and will therefore give preference to suitably qualified candidates who are members of designated groups. Would you like to take a tour to “Life at Ericsson MEA”, visit short video Click Here
   

  

**Why join Ericsson?**
 At Ericsson, you´ll have an outstanding opportunity. The chance to use your skills and imagination to push the boundaries of what´s possible. To build solutions never seen before to some of the world’s toughest problems. You´ll be challenged, but you won’t be alone. You´ll be joining a team of diverse innovators, all driven to go beyond the status quo to craft what comes next.
   

  

**What happens once you apply?**
 Click Here to find all you need to know about what our typical hiring process looks like.
   

  

 Encouraging a diverse and inclusive organization is core to our values at Ericsson, that's why we champion it in everything we do. We truly believe that by collaborating with people with different experiences we drive innovation, which is essential for our future growth. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply and realize their full potential as part of our Ericsson team. Ericsson is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. learn more.
   

  

**Req ID:** 
 776664

data.title: Head of Distribution
data.raw_snippet:
**Job Purpose** 




  





 Head of Distribution is responsible for leading and managing the overall Retail Banking Distribution Network, including branches, sales channels, and distribution support functions. The role drives the strategic direction, performance, and profitability of retail distri
data.jd_text:
**Job Purpose** 




  





 Head of Distribution is responsible for leading and managing the overall Retail Banking Distribution Network, including branches, sales channels, and distribution support functions. The role drives the strategic direction, performance, and profitability of retail distribution channels, ensuring effective customer acquisition, service delivery, and market expansion.
 



  





 The position ensures alignment between Retail Banking strategy, branch performance, and channel optimization while maintaining high standards of governance, operational efficiency, and customer experience across the distribution network.
 



  





**Key responsibilities of the role include:** 




  




* Development and execution of the Retail Distribution Strategy aligned with Retail Banking objectives.
* Driving sales performance, customer acquisition, and revenue growth across the branch network.
* Optimizing branch network footprint and multi\-channel distribution.
* Strengthening customer experience and operational efficiency.
* Ensuring strong governance, compliance, and Sharia adherence across all distribution activities.



  





  





**Main Tasks \& Accountabilities** 




  





**Distribution Strategy \& Business Growth** 




  




* sustainable growth within Retail Banking.
* Translate strategic objectives into clear distribution frameworks and execution plans across the branch network and sales channels.
* Ensure alignment between distribution strategy, market opportunities, and overall Retail Banking objectives.
* Continuously review and refine distribution approaches to ensure the organization remains competitive and responsive to market dynamics.



  





**Channel Optimization \& Network Management** 




  




* Lead and oversee the activities of Retail Banking sales and distribution channels.
* Ensure optimal utilization and coordination of distribution channels to support customer acquisition and service delivery.
* Develop and execute the branch network strategy, including expansion, relocation, consolidation, or optimization of branches where required.
* Monitor the overall effectiveness of the distribution network and identify opportunities to improve reach and efficiency.



  





**Operational Effectiveness \& Customer Experience** 




  




* Coordinate initiatives aimed at enhancing operational efficiency across distribution channels.
* Promote a culture focused on service quality and continuous improvement in customer experience.
* Support the implementation of initiatives that enhance the effectiveness and responsiveness of the distribution network.



  





**Stakeholder Engagement \& Governance** 




  




* Strengthen relationships with internal stakeholders and external partners to support distribution effectiveness.
* Ensure adherence to organizational policies, regulatory requirements, and governance standards across distribution activities.
* Facilitate coordination between distribution teams and other business units to ensure alignment between strategy and execution.



  





**Leadership Responsibilities** 




  




* Inspire and lead distribution teams towards high levels of performance and accountability.
* Build and sustain a high\-performing distribution organization through effective leadership and talent development.
* Foster strong working relationships across the organization to support business objectives and collaboration.
* Ensure that operational activities within the distribution network remain aligned with strategic priorities.



  





**Education Level** 




  




* Minimum Bachelor’s Degree in Banking, Finance, Business Administration or related field



  





**Professional Qualifications** 




  




* Professional banking or leadership certifications (preferred)



  





**Work Experience** 




  




* Minimum 12–15 years of banking experience, with at least 8 years in Retail Banking distribution or sales leadership roles.
* Proven experience in managing branch networks and multi\-channel distribution.
* Strong track record in retail sales growth, customer acquisition, and deposit mobilization.
* Experience in strategic planning, distribution management, and business performance optimization.
* Sound knowledge of Oman retail banking market and competitive landscape.
* Knowledge of Islamic Banking and Sharia principles.
* Good understanding of CBO regulations and Oman banking environment.



  





  





**General Competencies** 




  




* Strong leadership and team management capabilities.
* Excellent strategic planning and execution skills.
* Strong communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.
* Customer\-centric and performance\-driven mindset.
* High standards of integrity, governance, and compliance.

data.title: Head of Custody Operations
data.raw_snippet:
A leading asset management firm based in Muscat is seeking a highly experienced
 **Head of Custody Operations** 
 to lead and transform its custody function. This is a senior leadership role with full responsibility for overseeing custody operations, ensuring best\-in\-class service delivery, robust
data.jd_text:
A leading asset management firm based in Muscat is seeking a highly experienced
 **Head of Custody Operations** 
 to lead and transform its custody function. This is a senior leadership role with full responsibility for overseeing custody operations, ensuring best\-in\-class service delivery, robust risk management, and regulatory compliance across global markets.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 




**Operational Leadership** 



* Lead and manage the custody operations function, including settlement, corporate actions, income processing, and reconciliations
* Define and execute operational strategy aligned with business objectives
* Ensure seamless daily processing of securities transactions across international markets



  





**Securities Settlement \& Asset Servicing** 



* Oversee end\-to\-end trade settlement across asset classes (equities, fixed income, derivatives)
* Manage corporate actions lifecycle (dividends, rights issues, mergers, stock splits, etc.)
* Ensure accurate income processing and entitlement distribution



  





**Risk Management \& Controls** 



* Establish and maintain strong operational controls to mitigate risk
* Oversee reconciliations across cash, securities, and client accounts
* Lead incident management and continuous risk improvement initiatives



  





**Regulatory Compliance** 



* Ensure full compliance with local and international regulatory frameworks
* Maintain adherence to standards such as SWIFT messaging and client asset protection rules
* Support internal and external audits



  





**Client Service \& Relationship Management** 



* Build and maintain relationships with institutional clients (asset managers, pension funds, sovereign entities)
* Ensure high\-quality service delivery and timely issue resolution
* Partner with client\-facing teams to enhance overall client experience



  





**Technology \& Process Improvement** 



* Drive automation and digital transformation initiatives
* Identify opportunities to improve efficiency and reduce operational costs
* Collaborate with IT on custody platforms and system enhancements



  





**Team Leadership** 



* Lead, mentor, and develop a team of custody professionals
* Set clear KPIs and performance standards
* Foster a culture of accountability and operational excellence



  





**Candidate Profile** 




**Education** 



* Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Banking, Business Administration, or related field
* Advanced qualifications (CFA, FRM, or similar) preferred



  





**Experience** 



* Minimum 10 years’ experience in custody, securities services, or investment operations
* At least 5–7 years in a leadership role
* Strong expertise in settlement, corporate actions, and asset servicing



  





**Key Skills** 



* Custody operations leadership
* Securities settlement and clearing
* Corporate actions and asset servicing
* Operational risk and controls
* Regulatory compliance
* Process improvement and automation
* Stakeholder and client relationship management



  





**Why Apply?** 




 This is a high\-impact leadership role offering the opportunity to shape and modernize a critical function within a growing asset management business in the region. You will play a key role in driving operational excellence and supporting the firm’s long\-term growth strategy.

data.title: Director - Wholesale Banking
data.raw_snippet:
**JOB PURPOSE:** 




 To originate, manage, and grow a high‑quality corporate banking portfolio by developing deep client relationships, delivering strategic financial solutions, and coordinating with product partners across FAB. The role ensures strong risk management, portfolio quality, revenue g
data.jd_text:
**JOB PURPOSE:** 




 To originate, manage, and grow a high‑quality corporate banking portfolio by developing deep client relationships, delivering strategic financial solutions, and coordinating with product partners across FAB. The role ensures strong risk management, portfolio quality, revenue growth, and adherence to CBO regulatory frameworks i.e., BM 1108, BM 1170 and all other regulatory circulars issued by CBO. The Director acts as a primary client advisor and portfolio owner, responsible for end‑to‑end execution on credit, onboarding, cross‑sell, and service delivery.
 



  





**KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES:** 



* **Client Relationship \& Portfolio Management:**
* Lead coverage for corporates and Business Banking clients, in country and across FAB network.
* Develop Account Plans, grow wallet share, enhance cross‑sell.
* Ensure portfolio monitoring, early‑warning identification, compliance with FAB’s credit risk appetite.
* Maintain credit quality and ensure all reviews, renewals, and covenants are completed.
* Examine \& resolve credit, market and operational risk aspects of the transactions in collaboration with products and enablement units.
* Prepare Key Account Plans for clients.



  




* **Origination \& Deal Execution:**
* Identify, structure, and originate opportunities aligned with client needs.
* Prepare CIMs, term sheets, pricing proposals.
* Lead end‑to‑end execution with Credit, Risk, Legal, Ops, and product partners.
* Manage P\&L responsibility of the assigned portfolio, achieving targets within defined budget levels.



  




* **Risk Management \& Compliance:**
* Maintain strong credit discipline and covenant monitoring.
* Ensure AML/KYC compliance, periodic reviews, and regulatory adherence.
* Support internal/external audits, CBO examination, ORM, BCP, and internal compliance.
* Manage and ensure effective implementation of the business functional policies, procedures and controls covering all areas of assigned department activity so that all relevant procedural/legislative requirements, fulfilled while delivering a quality, cost\-effective service.



  




* **Collaboration with Product Partners/Sector Heads:**
* Work closely with GTx, Trade, Markets, Treasury, Corporate Finance, etc and all other product partners and sector heads/team.
* Drive cross‑sell revenue and support NTB onboarding.
* Promote digital adoption across FAB corporate channels.



  




* **Market \& Sector Intelligence:**
* Conduct sector analysis and provide insights into management.
* Track economic, sovereign, GRE, and market developments.



  




* **Operational Excellence:**
* Ensure documentation, KFS, account plans, and pipeline accuracy.
* Adhere to governance frameworks and DOA.



  




* **Day‑to‑Day Activities:**
* Manage daily client engagement and transactions end‑to‑end.
* Update CRM, MIS, LH pipeline trackers regularly.
* Assist in the preparation of timely and accurate statements and reports to meet the unit’s requirements, policies, and standards.
* Learning \& Development – Attend appropriate on and off\-the\-job training programs and ensure development and skill upgradation of team members.
* Support ED/Coverage Head with portfolio and governance reports.



  





**QUALIFICATIONS \& EXPERIENCE:** 




**Minimum Qualification:** 



* Bachelor’s degree in finance, Banking, Economics, Business, or similar
* MBA/Finance preferred.



**Minimum Experience:** 



* 5\+ years of experience in Corporate Banking or a similar area preferably managing client relationships.



  





**Knowledge, Skills, and Attributes:** 



* Strong understanding of corporate banking products and credit structuring.
* Familiarity with CBO frameworks including BM1108, BM1170, KYC/AML, KFS.

data.title: Segment Solution Technology Lead - Healthcare
data.raw_snippet: Schneider Electric is a global leader in energy management, industrial automation, and digital transformation technologies, operating in more than 100 countries. We believe that access to energy and digital technology is a fundamental human right. Our purpose is to empower everyone to make the most
data.jd_text:
Schneider Electric is a global leader in energy management, industrial automation, and digital transformation technologies, operating in more than 100 countries. We believe that access to energy and digital technology is a fundamental human right. Our purpose is to empower everyone to make the most of their energy and resources, bridging progress and sustainability for all.
   

  

**Our mission** 
 is to be the
 **Energy Technology Partner** 
 for our customers \- leading the electrification, automation, and digitalization of every industry, business, and home to drive efficiency, resilience, and sustainability. This mission builds on our commitment to act as our customers’ best digital partner for sustainability and efficiency by integrating advanced energy technologies, digital solutions, and domain expertise.
   

  

 We drive digital transformation by integrating world\-leading energy and automation technologies. This includes:
   

  

* End\-to\-end integration, from connected products to edge control and cloud applications.
* Lifecycle services, from design and build to operate and maintain, powered by digital twins and AI\-enabled insights.
* Enterprise\-wide optimization, transforming operations from site\-level to multi\-site organizational performance.
* Integrated platforms, including EcoStruxure™, enabling safe, reliable, efficient, and cybersecure environments across buildings, homes, data centers, infrastructure, and industry
* Description of the role \& responsibilities of the Segment Solution and Technology Lead


 The Segment Solution and Technology Lead is a role, with a specific intent to focus on complex digital building applications in the Healthcare sector. The role functionally reports into the Building Segment Solutions Director as part of the solutions specialist’s team with the mandate to focus on all opportunities involving complex IT/OT architectures across the Healthcare Segment.
   

  

 The role plays a critical role in advancing digital transformation across healthcare facilities by shaping solution architectures, driving innovation with the Line of Business (LoB), and supporting strategic customer engagements. This role blends technical depth, account leadership, and solution evangelism, ensuring Schneider Electric delivers differentiated value in healthcare environments.
   

  

 You will act as a trusted advisor to healthcare customers, guiding them through IT/OT convergence, energy and operational efficiency improvements, and the adoption of advanced digital building technologies.
   

  

 The Healthcare Segment Solution and Technology Lead will be the catalyst and champion of innovation for Healthcare facilities and will be responsible for driving innovation, and influencing and supporting strategic customers in the digitalization of their buildings’ portfolio.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities And Deliverables**
 Drive Innovation \& Collaboration with Line of Business (LoB)
   

  

* Serve as a catalyst for innovation across healthcare solutions by working closely with LoB leaders, R\&D, offer managers, and solution owners to shape new offers and solution architectures.
* Contribute to the development of healthcare‑specific solution roadmaps, use cases, and reference architectures.
* Advocate for segment needs and ensure healthcare requirements influence future products and platform evolution.
* Participate in solution catalog development, documentation refinement, and strategic solution governance activities.


 Customer \& Account Leadership
   

  

* Act as a trusted technology partner for healthcare customers, advising on digital transformation, modernization, and resilience strategies.
* Support strategic pursuits across major healthcare accounts in coordination with Account Managers, Segment Leaders, and Solution Architects.
* Manage executive‑level relationships and position Schneider Electric as a visionary and value‑oriented partner.
* Influence specifications and consultant ecosystems to shape demand and steer customer decisions in Schneider Electric’s favor. Insights from healthcare team interactions and consultant influence discussions support this (e.g., Healthcare Solutions Community


 Solution Architecture \& Technical Leadership
   

  

* Lead the definition of end‑to‑end IT/OT architectures for healthcare facilities, including data strategy, integration, system interoperability, and analytics requirements.
* Shape conceptual and high‑level solution designs using Schneider Electric’s digital platforms, energy technologies, software, and services.
* Ensure alignment with healthcare‑specific needs such as critical environments, safety, compliance, and cybersecurity.
* Support complex deals by preparing architectural deliverables, technical value propositions, and solution narratives.


 Workshop Facilitation \& Technical Engagements
   

  

* Facilitate customer workshops covering digital building transformation, energy efficiency, FDD (fault detection diagnostics), IoT, and smart hospital solutions.
* Translate customer challenges into solution requirements and actionable architectural proposals.
* Present demos, reference architectures, and case studies to educate customers and internal teams.
* Bring in best practices from healthcare multi‑system design conversations and Building Advisor use cases mentioned in internal exchanges.
* Training / upskilling zones and country operations teams through healthcare segment specialisation training
* Upskilling teams to deliver partner / EcoXpert training


 Cross‑Functional Collaboration \& Ecosystem Enablement
   

  

* Work with regional and global stakeholders—Segment Leaders, Solution Architects, Marketing, LoBs, and Partner Ecosystems—to ensure solutions land successfully in the field.
* Provide guidance to local teams, acting as an advanced support and coaching resource, particularly in priority growth regions.
* Strengthen relationships with engineering firms, integrators, and consultants influencing healthcare projects.


 Thought Leadership \& Best Practice Development
   

  

* Create repeatable frameworks, templates, and best practices to scale Healthcare digital solutions globally.
* Partner with marketing and the segment team to build training materials, reference architectures, and whitepapers showcasing Schneider Electric’s leadership.
* Contribute content to internal platforms (e.g., Solutions Catalogue, Healthcare Playbooks) and support continuous improvement of documentation processes.


 Opportunity Support \& Deal Acceleration
   

  

* Support the end‑to‑end pursuit lifecycle: opportunity qualification, scoping, solutioning, proposal development, and closing.
* Help prioritize strategic accounts and segment opportunities based on market signals and portfolio strengths.
* Leverage deep knowledge of digital hospitals, energy management, microgrids, and hybrid cloud/on‑prem architectures to accelerate the design of winning offers.
* Requirements for the role
* Customer\-centric mindset: excellent customer relationship skills. Ability to create relationships quickly across customers’ organizations.
* 10\+ years experience within the Healthcare Segment proofing her/his knowledge in this field
* Practical experience working in the Healthcare environment, with experience in deploying complex multi\-site software solutions with a wide range of integrations
* Awareness of RESTful standards and web API's and good knowledge of standard building protocols
* Good understanding of networks and network design
* Experience in the development and influence of specifications to embed software in project requirements
* Strong capacity to foster collaboration \& networking, and work transversally across the organization
* Structured way of working; Good attention to detail
* Confident speaker able to lead technical workshops with customers
* International, multi\-cultural mind\-set and approach
* Business Developer \- Resourceful and self\-starter attitude, interest to roll\-up one sleeves, get things done and push the organization to deliver
* Team Player \- Passionate and dynamic professional with the energy and ability to build trust and foster collaboration across a complex matrix environment; Collaborative team\-oriented style.


 Role Model \- Demonstrates strong personal leadership in line with Schneider Electric values.
   

  

* 10 \+ years of experience in Healthcare
* Good knowledge and practical experience in executing or providing design consultancy in building automation and energy management systems, enterprise systems and systems integration
* Good knowledge of electrical distribution with experience in distributed energy resources and microgrids
* Excellent networking capabilities and the ability to lead other teams and groups.
* Market: experience within the Healthcare industry
* Education: Bachelor’s degree, Masters’ degree in a relevant field, Computer Engineering or Computer Sciences is a plus
* Languages: Fluency in spoken and written English; Any other language is a plus
* Other: excellent MS PowerPoint and Excel skills, and presentation writing skills


**Looking to make an IMPACT with your career?**
 When you are thinking about joining a new team, culture matters. At Schneider Electric, our values and behaviors are the foundation for creating a great culture to support business success. We believe that our IMPACT values – Inclusion, Mastery, Purpose, Action, Curiosity, Teamwork – starts with us.
   

  

 IMPACT is also your invitation to join Schneider Electric where you can contribute to turning sustainability ambition into actions, no matter what role you play. It is a call to connect your career with the ambition of achieving a more resilient, efficient, and sustainable world.
   

  

 We are looking for IMPACT Makers; exceptional people who turn sustainability ambitions into actions at the intersection of automation, electrification, and digitization. We celebrate IMPACT Makers and believe everyone has the potential to be one.
   

  

 Become an IMPACT Maker with Schneider Electric – apply today!
   

  

 €36 billion global revenue
   

  

 \+13% organic growth
   

  

 150 000\+ employees in 100\+ countries
   

  

 \#1 on the Global 100 World’s most sustainable corporations
   

  

 You must submit an online application to be considered for any position with us. This position will be posted until filled.
   

  

*Schneider Electric aspires to be the most inclusive and caring company in the world, by providing equitable opportunities to everyone, everywhere, and ensuring all employees feel uniquely valued and safe to contribute their best. We mirror the diversity of the communities in which we operate, and ‘inclusion’ is one of our core values. We believe our differences make us stronger as a company and as individuals and we are committed to championing inclusivity in everything we do.*
*At Schneider Electric, we uphold the highest standards of ethics and compliance, and we believe that trust is a foundational value. Our Trust Charter is our Code of Conduct and demonstrates our commitment to ethics, safety, sustainability, quality and cybersecurity, underpinning every aspect of our business and our willingness to behave and respond respectfully and in good faith to all our stakeholders. You can find out more about our Trust Charter here*
*Schneider Electric is an Equal Opportunity Employer. It is our policy to provide equal employment and advancement opportunities in the areas of recruiting, hiring, training, transferring, and promoting all qualified individuals regardless of race, religion, color, gender, disability, national origin, ancestry, age, military status, sexual orientation, marital status, or any other legally protected characteristic or conduct.*

 DEAL Holdings is building a best
data.jd_text:
Location: Dubai, UAE
   

  

 Reports to: Financial Controller / Group CFO / Group CEO
   

  

 Department: Central Services \- Finance
   

  

 Industry: Hospitality / Retail / Wellness / Lifestyle
   

  

 Employment Type: Full\-time
   

  

**Role Overview**
 DEAL Holdings is building a best\-in\-class finance function to support a fast\-scaling, multi\-brand portfolio across hospitality, wellness, retail, and lifestyle sectors.
   

  

 We are hiring across two levels within the finance function:
   

  

 Head of Finance (strategic leadership role)
   

  

 Finance Manager (operational and build\-focused role)
   

  

 This role sits at the centre of the business during its setup and expansion phase, with responsibility extending beyond traditional finance into systems, structure, governance, and cross\-functional integration.
   

  

 You will work closely with PMO, Operations, and Central Services, supporting new openings, project delivery, and long\-term scale.
   

  

 We are open to candidates at different levels of seniority, provided they bring strong SME experience, system implementation capability, and hands\-on execution.
   

  

 This is a build\-and\-lead finance role, combining:
   

  

* Financial operations and reporting
* Department setup and structure design
* Systems implementation and integration
* Project finance and PMO coordination
* Commercial and operational support
* Depending on experience, you will either:


 Lead and shape the finance function at group level, or dive execution, setup, and day\-to\-day operations within a growing structure.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities** 
**10\. Business Partnering** 
**B. Pre\-Opening \& Launch Support** 
**C. Scale\-Up \& Expansion**
* Finance Operations \& Control
* Oversee full accounting cycle: GL, AP/AR, payroll, treasury, reconciliations
* Lead month\-end, quarter\-end, and year\-end close across multiple entities
* Prepare consolidated financials and management reporting
* Implement and enforce financial controls, approval matrices, and reporting standards
* Budgeting, Planning \& Commercial Support
* Lead budgeting, forecasting, and reforecast cycles
* Develop financial models, feasibility studies, and scenario planning for new concepts
* Conduct variance analysis and provide actionable insights
* Support pricing, menu engineering, and cost optimisation across F\&B operations
* Tax, Compliance \& Governance
* Ensure compliance with UAE VAT, corporate tax, ESR, and IFRS
* Manage audits, filings, and regulatory requirements
* Establish internal controls, SOPs, and governance frameworks across all entities
* Cash Flow, Treasury \& Banking
* Oversee group\-wide cash flow planning and working capital management
* Lead setup and management of bank accounts across multiple entities
* Establish payment processes, signatory structures, and banking mandates
* Manage relationships with banks, lenders, and financial institutions
* Implement robust cash control and liquidity frameworks
* ERP, POS \& Financial Systems
* Lead implementation and optimisation of ERP and financial systems
* Ensure full integration across POS, inventory, procurement, and accounting systems
* Develop scalable reporting tools and dashboards
* Train teams on systems and financial processes
* Procurement, Purchasing \& Cost Control Oversight
* Oversee governance across procurement and purchasing functions
* Implement supplier onboarding, approval hierarchies, and purchasing controls
* Ensure integration between procurement, inventory, and finance systems
* Monitor supplier terms, pricing, and cost efficiency across all units
* Support margin optimisation and operational cost control
* Accounting Structure \& Financial Framework Setup
* Design and implement the full accountancy structure across all entities
* Develop and standardise the Chart of Accounts
* Establish reporting templates, consolidation frameworks, and intercompany structures
* Ensure scalability for multi\-brand and multi\-entity expansion
* Administration, PRO \& Corporate Structuring Oversight
* Provide financial oversight across administration and PRO functions
* Support company formation, trade licences, regulatory registrations, and renewals
* Ensure alignment between corporate structuring, licensing, and financial setup
* Track and manage costs related to government fees, licences, and compliance
* PMO Liaison \& Project Finance / Controls
* Work closely with the PMO and Project Delivery teams on all active developments
* Oversee financial management of projects, including budget tracking and cost control
* Support tender processes, bid evaluations, and commercial reviews
* Establish and manage project payment processes, including certifications and approvals
* Control financial documentation related to projects, contracts, and variations
* Monitor project cash flow, capex spend, and financial risk
* Ensure alignment between project delivery timelines and financial reporting
* Partner with Operations, PMO, HR, Procurement, and Brand teams
* Provide P\&L oversight and performance analysis across outlets and concepts
* Support new openings, commercial decisions, and strategic initiatives
* Evaluate investments, JV structures, and expansion opportunities
* Finance Function Setup \& Scale
* Foundation Phase
* Build finance department structure, roles, and reporting lines
* Establish SOPs, workflows, and approval frameworks
* Set up financial systems, banking structures, and reporting templates
* Recruit, onboard, and train finance and admin teams
* Lead financial setup for new outlets (costing, POS setup, reporting structures)
* Implement outlet\-level reporting from day one
* Support stocktakes, inventory controls, and operational stabilisation
* Prepare finance function for multi\-brand, multi\-entity growth
* Standardise reporting, controls, and cost structures across all units
* Support long\-term strategic planning and regional expansion


**Candidate Profile**
 We are intentionally open to both senior and mid\-level candidates, but the following is essential:
   

  

* Strong experience in SME or high\-growth environments
* Background in hospitality, F\&B, retail, or multi\-unit operations
* Proven ability to build systems, structures, and teams from scratch
* Hands\-on experience with ERP implementation and process design
* Experience working with PMO and project\-driven environments
* Strong understanding of UAE financial regulations and compliance frameworks
* Commercially minded with strong attention to detail and ownership


**Experience Guidelines**
 Finance Manager: 7\-10 years experience
   

  

 Head of Finance: 10\+ years with leadership and build experience
   

  

**Qualifications:**
 Degree in Finance / Accounting
   

  

 ACCA / CPA / CA preferred
   

  

**Why This Role**
* Opportunity to build a finance function from the ground up
* Direct involvement in projects, openings, and expansion strategy
* Exposure to a diversified, multi\-brand platform
* High level of ownership, autonomy, and impact


**Candidate Requirements**
* Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field; ACCA / CPA / CA preferred.
* 7–10 years of multi\-entity finance experience, ideally within hospitality, F\&B, retail, or lifestyle sectors.
* Strong experience in department setup, ERP implementation, and pre\-opening finance.
* Deep understanding of UAE VAT, corporate tax, IFRS, WPS, and governance frameworks.
* Proven leadership experience managing finance teams and cross\-functional stakeholders.
* High proficiency in Excel and financial modelling.
* Ability to work in fast\-paced environments with strong ownership and accuracy.
* Excellent communication, analytical, and problem\-solving skills.
